dialogos.texto_en: Hey can I talk to you for a bit?
Huh? It's Futaro-kun again. What is it this time?
Actually, I have something I want to ask Miku.
What? Me?
Do you have anything you want to do after this, Miku?\nSightseeing, shopping, anything is fine.
Yotsuba wants to know everyone's thoughts.
Ah, I see.\nThat's just like Yotsuba.
I don't really have anything in mind...\nanything is fine. Just tell Yotsuba to decide.
Hey, Futaro-kun. It's nice that you're asking\nfor our thoughts, but in the end, it's up to\nyou and Yotsuba to decide based on what you like, okay?
I think I agree with Ichika on this one.
Got it. I'll let Yotsuba know.
Oh, Futaro?\nDo you still have something to ask?
Yeah, I wanted to ask Ichika something.
Me?
Is there anything that\nyou want to do after this, Ichika?\nSightseeing, shopping, anything is fine.
Yotsuba wants to know what everyone wants to do.
Ah I see.
Hmm...\nHow about parasailing again?
You really liked that too, huh, Ichika.
It felt great to fly through the sky.\nI'm okay with switching partners too.
Hey, Futaro-kun. It's nice that you're asking\nfor our thoughts, but in the end, it's up to\nyou and Yotsuba to decide based on what you like, okay?
I think I agree with Ichika on this one.
Got it. I'll let Yotsuba know.
Oh, Futaro?\nWhat are you doing by yourself?
I've been looking for you guys,\nI had something I wanted to ask you.
Huh? Coming all the way to see us?\nIs something the matter?
It's nothing major.\nDo you guys have anything you want to do after this?
Why are you asking us?
Yotsuba wants to know everyone's thoughts,\nso I thought I would ask.
I see, so that's what's going on.
Hey, Futaro-kun. It's nice that you're asking\nfor our thoughts, but in the end, it's up to\nyou and Yotsuba to decide based on what you like, okay?
I think I agree with Ichika on this one.
Got it. I'll let Yotsuba know.
Well, to be honest,\neverything we could do seems like fun.
Yeah, even just swimming in the beautiful ocean\nis a lot of fun%p-2;――
Ah, but jellyfish are scary.
Jellyfish? Are there any here?
No, but it would be an issue if there were.
Are you afraid of jellyfish, Miku?
Not really, but there was a sign that said\nthere were dangerous ones around.
I think it was the habu-jellyfish and the man o' war.\nThey both have venom.
There's a jellyfish called the habu-jellyfish?\nI've heard of a snake called a habu,\nbut not a jellyfish.
The name of the habu-jellyfish\ncomes from the habu snake.\nIt's a jellyfish with venom just that strong.
Both stings are supposed to be painful.\nAnd, they say where you get stung swells up a lot.
Be careful, okay?\nI've heard that in the worst case,\nyou could end up in the hospital.
Aaaah...\nI definitely want to avoid that during this trip.
Well, jellyfish should only appear from around May.
Besides, this beach has jellyfish prevention nets\ninstalled, so there's no need to worry too much.
The nets are those things out in the ocean, right?
Oh, I see. Those are for jellyfish. That's a relief.
Jellyfish in aquariums\nare beautiful and soothing to watch,\nbut there are also dangerous ones among them, huh.
You think watching jellyfish is soothing?
Isn't it relaxing just to watch them drift leisurely?\nI think there's a healing effect to it.
Well, but if you look closely,\njellyfish are kinda gross, aren't they?
Some people might feel that way.
If that's the case,\nthen just don't look at them too closely.
For the sake of one another, appreciate their beauty\nby squinting your eyes and talking about how prettily\nthey flutter around. Yeah, that'd be nice.
What are you going on about...?
In any case, if you want to see jellyfish safely,\ngoing to an aquarium is probably the best option.
What should we do?\nShould we add it to the list of things we want to do?
Hmm?\nWasn't a trip to the aquarium\nalready on the schedule somewhere?
They may look pretty, but just be sure\nnot to touch them. Even the ones washed up on the beach.
They're probably dead anyway,\nand even then you can't touch them?
Even if the jellyfish itself is dead,\nif you touch the tentacles,\nthey'll sting you out of reflex.
Wow, really?\nThat's freaky.
I'll tell everyone else, too.
Yeah.\nIt wouldn't be funny if our trip\nwas ruined by jellyfish.
In any case, if you want to see jellyfish safely,\ngoing to an aquarium is probably the best option.
What should we do?\nShould we add it to the list of things we want to do?
Hmm?\nWasn't a trip to the aquarium\nalready on the schedule somewhere?
Aquariums are designed for them.\nThey use lighting cleverly to make them\nlook beautiful, don't they.
Jellyfish are transparent, so you can create\nmany different effects with lighting colors and angles.
Thinking about it,\nit's similar to photography.\nThe image can change completely with just one light.
Is that so.
We take many shots using various lighting\nto match the cameraman's image.\nAs an actress, we have to match that too.
Wow, actresses have it tough too.
Well, it's not that tough.\nIt's our job, and they're trying to bring out\nour good side.
I see.\nSo, you take many shots because\nthere's joy in bringing out everyone's good side.
That's right!\nIt isn't super serious though.
In any case, if you want to see jellyfish safely,\ngoing to an aquarium is probably the best option.
What should we do?\nShould we add it to the list of things we want to do?
Hmm?\nWasn't a trip to the aquarium\nalready on the schedule somewhere?
Oh, right, we're going to Ocean Expo Park.\nThat's where Churaumi Aquarium is.
Then it seems like\nwe'll be able to see some\njellyfish after all.
Then, that should be plenty for us.\nWe'll leave it up to you and Yotsuba\nto decide what to do, Futaro-kun.
Got it. I'll let Yotsuba know.
